title: crepis ciliata
intro: Crepis ciliata, also known as fringed hawk's-beard or fringed Hawksbeard, is a flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and is valued for its potential to help with digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The root of the Crepis ciliata plant can be used to make herbal teas and infusions, which are said to have a number of health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Additionally, the plant's roots can be dried and made into powder or capsules for further preparation and consumption as a dietary supplement.
